text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 March 2017 

Dr Patel  
St Mary's Hospital  
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Emily Carter, DOB: 17-08-1989, Hospital No: C672154 NHS No. 4728395612  
78 Willow Lane, London, W9 3HT 

Medication: 

Oxcarbazepine (Trileptal) 600 mg morning and 600 mg night. No changes made today.

Thank you for reviewing this patient who attended neurology clinic reporting steady improvement in day-to-day control. She has focused on regularising her routine, and her sleep hygiene has improved with a more consistent bedtime and wake time; her sleep pattern appears more stable. Prior to this improvement, she described cluster days twice this month, typically two seizures in 24 hours on those days. Over the last several weeks she has noticed that episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis, and some months have passed without any events at all. This monthly pattern is patient-reported and coincides with the improvements in sleep and overall routine.

She has not reported new adverse effects on the current dose. There have been no witnessed convulsions, injuries, or emergency attendances. She has kept a diary, which corroborates the reduction in frequency and the emerging monthly pattern. Triggers previously suspected (fatigue and irregular sleep) have been addressed through the improved sleep routine. She is comfortable to continue with the current plan and understands to seek advice if the pattern changes or if events recur more than monthly.

Plan:
- Continue Oxcarbazepine 600 mg twice daily unchanged.
- Maintain current sleep hygiene measures as they appear beneficial.
- Continue seizure diary to monitor for any deviation from the current monthly pattern.
- Routine follow-up in six months, earlier if clusters return or if frequency increases.

We will see her back in six months.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states the patient previously had cluster days twice in the month (two seizures in 24 hours on those days). More recently: "episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis, and some months have passed without any events at all." The plan and diary corroborate a current monthly pattern. Not seizure-free for 6 months, so we label current frequency. The highest frequency mentioned refers to earlier in the same month (two cluster days), but the letter frames that as prior to improvement; the current pattern is monthly at most. No concurrent multiple frequencies to sum. Therefore, normalize to 1 per month.
seizure_frequency_number: episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis
